message:
chore(beads): update ft-3681t.1.5.1 with delivery notes and blocked-env label

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.3","title":"[design] FrankenTerm convergence architecture spec (native mux + connector fabric)","description":"Author the definitive target architecture for FrankenTerm as the unified swarm terminal/control plane: native mux substrate, orchestration kernel, robot APIs, connector fabric integration, policy/governance plane, and observability model. This is the north-star contract for all downstream implementation beads.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Architecture document defines components, interfaces, data flows, and ownership boundaries. 2. NTM parity and connector integration strategies are explicit and testable. 3. Failure/rollback/degradation paths are built into the architecture, not deferred.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperCompass","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:28.044490Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:37:26.186866Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T23:37:26.186848Z","close_reason":"Authored convergence architecture baseline in docs/ft-3681t-convergence-architecture.md and linked from docs/architecture.md; includes topology, contracts, failure/degradation/rollback matrix, downstream bead mapping, and validation gates. Explicit merge points for ft-3681t.1.1/.1.2 inventories documented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design","fcp-integration","ntm-convergence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:28.044490Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:09.913544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:13.103361Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.4","title":"[program] Execution tracks, critical path, and measurable success gates","description":"Translate architecture into executable delivery tracks with dependency graph, sequencing, milestones, and measurable success thresholds (performance, reliability, governance, migration confidence). Include anti-goals to avoid partial/incremental dead-ends.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Critical path and parallelizable tracks are explicitly defined. 2. Each major deliverable has measurable go/no-go gates. 3. Plan supports swarm execution without ambiguity.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.

 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.5","title":"[quality] Capability/architecture traceability verification pack","description":"Produce a traceability pack proving every NTM and flywheel_connectors capability maps to explicit FrankenTerm design decisions, risks, and implementation beads.","acceptance_criteria":"1. +{"id":"ft-3681t.1.5.1","title":"[traceability] Refresh implementation-anchor matrix and verification scaffold","description":"Update convergence traceability artifacts with current implementation anchors (CLI entrypoints, watcher/runtime loops, robot envelope/schema surfaces, storage/event/policy/workflow paths). Add a machine-checkable baseline matrix and lightweight verification checks/scripts that can be extended when ft-3681t.1.1 and ft-3681t.1.2 finalize.","notes":"Traceability artifacts + validation scaffold delivered. Remaining closure blocker: remote-only workspace gates via rch are failing on worker env (missing cairo.pc for cairo-sys-rs in workspace check) and rch 300s timeout can fail-open local; local fallbacks were interrupted. Using TMPDIR=/tmp mitigates ControlPath-too-long sync failures.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonGlacier","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:42:52.829733Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:58:06.145649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","blocked-env","quality-gate","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:42:52.829733Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2","title":"[epic] Native mux supersystem for swarm-scale orchestration","description":"# Epic 2: Native Mux Supersystem

Implement the native mux substrate required to subsume NTM behavior with deeper platform leverage than tmux-based orchestration can provide.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Native mux covers session/window/pane lifecycle and orchestration primitives required by NTM workflows.
2. Durable snapshot/rollback and remote/headless control are production-ready.
3. Behavioral invariants are testable via replay and integration scenarios.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:41.725788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:30:47.526614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","native-mux","orchestration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:41.725788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:48.115955Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1719,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design note: Native mux is treated as a strategic moat, not a compatibility layer. We are intentionally absorbing NTM functionality into mux-adjacent primitives (lifecycle, topology, transport, persistence, headless control) so higher layers become simpler and safer.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2.1","title":"[native-mux] Core lifecycle engine for sessions/windows/panes","description":"Implement the canonical native mux lifecycle model that replaces tmux assumptions: creation, teardown, ownership, metadata, and stable identities for sessions, windows, panes, and agents. Ensure it is designed for orchestration and connector-driven automation from day one.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lifecycle APIs cover all primitives needed by orchestration and robot control. 2. Identity model is stable and globally addressable. 3. Concurrency and recovery semantics are specified and tested.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperCompass","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:32.091658Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:14.311753Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:14.311664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","native-mux","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:21.137793Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:32.091658Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1753,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","author":"CopperCompass","text":"Implemented lifecycle registry slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs plus integration tests.

What landed:
- Added canonical lifecycle union/state engine for session/window/pane/agent
- Added globally-addressable identity helpers (session/window/pane/agent)
- Added LifecycleRegistry with optimistic concurrency (expected_version) and recovery/state-transition semantics
- Added structured transition audit entries with timestamp/component/correlation_id/scenario_id/input/output/decision/reason_code/error_code
- Added unit tests for happy/idempotent/recovery/conflict/invalid-context/bootstrap paths
- Added integration test file crates/frankenterm-core/tests/native_mux_lifecycle_integration.rs covering topology->registry bootstrap, disconnect/recover flow, and stale-writer degraded path.

Validation attempts (per AGENTS via rch):
- rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 <changed-files> (pass)
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-coppercompass cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test native_mux_lifecycle_integration -- --nocapture (remote connect failed -> local SIGTERM in build scripts)
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-coppercompass CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features (remote connect failed -> local run produced no final completion signal)
- ubs --only=rust --diff . (reported fmt/clippy/check clean in shadow diff scan; broad pre-existing warnings/criticals elsewhere in repo)
","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:21:10Z"},{"id":1754,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","author":"CopperCompass","text":"Follow-up slice landed:
- Added LifecycleRegistry JSON artifact helpers: transition_log_json() and snapshot_json().
- Added deterministic integration test scenario  in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/native_mux_lifecycle_integration.rs.
  - Includes explicit failure injection (stale writer/version conflict) and recovery.
  - Verifies structured artifact fields (,  via scenario string, , ).

Re-ran targeted rustfmt via  on changed files.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:24:35Z"},{"id":1755,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","author":"CopperCompass","text":"Follow-up slice landed:
- Added LifecycleRegistry JSON artifact helpers: `transition_log_json()` and `snapshot_json()`.
- Added deterministic integration test scenario `deterministic_e2e_failure_injection_then_recovery_emits_artifacts` in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/native_mux_lifecycle_integration.rs`.
  - Includes explicit failure injection (stale writer/version conflict) and recovery.
  - Verifies structured artifact fields (`correlation_id`, `scenario_id` via scenario string, `reason_code`, `error_code`).

Re-ran targeted rustfmt via `rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 <changed-files>` on changed files.
","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:24:50Z"},{"id":1757,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DustyFinch build verification: All 54 lifecycle tests pass (51 edge-case + 3 integration). Compiled clean.","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:03:04Z"},{"id":1758,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","author":"CopperCompass","text":"Fresh-eyes review pass completed for lifecycle slice in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs`.

Findings addressed:
1. Added regression coverage for duplicate pane rows during bootstrap so strongest state is preserved (`running` must not be downgraded to `ready` when duplicate rows exist).
2. Strengthened invalid-context behavior coverage: test now asserts rejected transition is logged with `native_mux.lifecycle.invalid_context` error code.

Validation attempts:
- `rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs`
- `rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/native_mux_lifecycle_integration.rs`
- targeted `rch exec -- cargo test ...` still affected by remote-connect fallback and long local compile runs in shared workspace.
","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:56:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2.2","title":"[native-mux] Topology and layout orchestration service","description":"Build programmable topology management (split/merge/rebalance/grid/focus groups/role-based layouts) to replace ad-hoc pane arrangement workflows and make fleet visual/state organization deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"1.
